data.description:
## Why
Code review (M3) flagged `shallow_contract_check` living in `executor.py` but
re-exported from `lint_refs.py` via `__getattr__` to dodge an import cycle (forced by
the lint_refs size cap), and that it is not yet consumed by the linter.

## What / Scope
Decide + record the home of `shallow_contract_check`: either relocate it to a neutral
module both `executor` and `lint_refs` can import directly (removing the `__getattr__`
indirection), or document the indirection as an accepted tradeoff. Wire it into the
linter if/where the static check belongs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `shallow_contract_check`'s placement is either cleaned up (no `__getattr__` re-export) or explicitly documented as intentional with the rationale.
- [ ] If it is meant to back a lint check, it is wired in with a test; if standalone, that is documented.
data.title: Resolve shallow_contract_check module placement / lint_refs size
